- Topics: real-time object detection, detector selection
- Organizations: Internal Research Team
- Concepts: latency budget, production constraints

Facts:
- The Internal Research Team authored a research note on June 1, 2026.
- The research note topic is real-time object detection.
- The team's current working hypothesis is that YOLO11 is the best object detector for their real-time use case.
- The production constraint is a strict latency budget.
- YOLO11 has been the default detector choice so far.
- The note records the belief about YOLO11 to track supporting and contradicting evidence from papers and experiments.
- The status of the research note is active.

# Research Note — Detector Choice for Real-Time Pipeline

Author: Internal Research Team
Date: 2026-06-01
Topic: real-time object detection

## Current working hypothesis

We currently believe that **YOLO11 is the best object detector for our
real-time use case**. Our production constraint is a strict latency budget, and
so far YOLO11 has been our default choice.

This note records that belief so we can track which evidence supports it and
which evidence contradicts it as we read more papers and run more experiments.

Status: active.

nodes.text:
This chunk is about:
- Products: YOLO11, YOLOv8
- People: Glenn Jocher, Ayush Chaurasia
- Topics: real-time object detection
- Concepts: anchor-free detection head, improved backbone, mAP@0.5 benchmark
- Datasets: COCO dataset

Facts:
- Glenn Jocher and Ayush Chaurasia authored YOLO11 in 2024.
- YOLO11 is a real-time object detector that focuses on real-time object detection.
- YOLO11 is derived from and builds on the earlier YOLOv8 architecture.
- YOLO11 uses an anchor-free detection head and an improved backbone.
- YOLO11 is trained and evaluated on the COCO dataset.
- YOLO11 is evaluated using the mAP@0.5 benchmark to measure both accuracy and latency.
- YOLO11 reaches 54.7 mAP on the COCO dataset while running at only 2ms latency per image.
- YOLO11 achieves state-of-the-art real-time performance.
- YOLO11 extends the YOLOv8 architecture with a refined head.

# YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms

Authors: Glenn Jocher, Ayush Chaurasia
Year: 2024
Topic: real-time object detection

## Abstract

We present YOLO11, a real-time object detector. YOLO11 is derived from and
builds on the earlier YOLOv8 architecture. We evaluate YOLO11 on the COCO
dataset using the mAP@0.5 benchmark.

## Method and Evaluation

YOLO11 uses an anchor-free detection head and an improved backbone. The model is
trained and evaluated on the COCO dataset. Using the mAP@0.5 benchmark, we
measure both accuracy and latency.

## Key finding

On COCO, YOLO11 reaches 54.7 mAP while running at only 2ms latency per image.
This result shows that YOLO11 achieves state-of-the-art real-time performance,
supporting the view that YOLO11 is an excellent choice for real-time use cases.

YOLO11 is derived from YOLOv8, extending its architecture with a refined head.

nodes.text:
This chunk is about:
- People: Maria Santos, Wei Chen, Jocher, Chaurasia
- Products: YOLO11
- Topics: real-time object detection, production latency, latency budgets
- Systems: transformer-based detectors

Facts:
- Maria Santos and Wei Chen authored a paper titled "Latency-First Detection in Production" in 2025 on real-time object detection.
- Jocher and Chaurasia published "YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms" in 2024.
- The 2025 paper builds on and cites the YOLO11 work by Jocher and Chaurasia from 2024.
- The study deploys and compares object detectors in a real-time serving environment under strict production latency budgets.
- YOLO11 is used as the production detector with end-to-end latency measurement under real-time budgets.
- YOLO11 is compared against transformer-based detectors in the study.
- YOLO11's low latency met the real-time budget in production.
- Transformer-based detectors did not fit the latency constraint in production.
- The authors conclude that YOLO11 is the best detector for latency-critical, real-time use cases.

# Latency-First Detection in Production

Authors: Maria Santos, Wei Chen
Year: 2025
Topic: real-time object detection

## Abstract

This paper studies object detection under strict production latency budgets. We
deploy and compare detectors in a real-time serving environment. We build on and
cite the YOLO11 work of Jocher and Chaurasia (2024) ("YOLO11: Real-Time
Detection at 2ms").

## Method

We use YOLO11 as our production detector and measure end-to-end latency under a
real-time budget. We compare against transformer-based detectors.

## Key finding

YOLO11's low latency met our real-time budget in production, whereas the
transformer-based detectors we tried did not fit the latency constraint. This
supports the conclusion that YOLO11 is the best detector for latency-critical,
real-time use cases.

Reference: see "YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms" (Jocher and Chaurasia, 2024).

nodes.text:
This chunk is about:
- Papers/Publications: RT-DETR, YOLO11
- Authors: Yian Zhao, Wenyu Lv, Jocher, Chaurasia
- Topics: real-time object detection, transformer architecture
- Systems/Models: RT-DETR, YOLO11
- Dataset: COCO
- Metrics: mAP@0.5

Facts:
- Jocher and Chaurasia published "YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms" in 2024.
- Yian Zhao and Wenyu Lv introduced RT-DETR in 2025 as a real-time detection transformer.
- RT-DETR uses a transformer-based architecture for real-time object detection.
- RT-DETR was trained and evaluated on the COCO dataset using the mAP@0.5 benchmark.
- RT-DETR was compared directly against YOLO11, which served as the primary baseline.
- RT-DETR exceeds YOLO11 in accuracy by 2.1 mAP points on COCO at comparable latency.
- RT-DETR's results contradict the claim that YOLO11 is the best detector for real-time use cases.
- RT-DETR achieves higher accuracy than YOLO11 without sacrificing real-time performance.

# RT-DETR: Transformers Beat YOLO on Accuracy

Authors: Yian Zhao, Wenyu Lv
Year: 2025
Topic: real-time object detection

## Abstract

We introduce RT-DETR, a real-time detection transformer. We evaluate RT-DETR on
the COCO dataset using the mAP@0.5 benchmark and compare it directly against
YOLO11. We reference the YOLO11 work ("YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms",
Jocher and Chaurasia, 2024) as our primary baseline.

## Method

RT-DETR uses a transformer-based architecture. We train and evaluate on the COCO
dataset and report mAP@0.5.

## Key finding

On COCO, RT-DETR exceeds YOLO11 in accuracy by 2.1 mAP points at a comparable
latency. This result directly contradicts the claim that YOLO11 is the best
detector for real-time use cases, because a transformer detector achieves higher
accuracy without sacrificing real-time performance.

Reference: "YOLO11: Real-Time Detection at 2ms" (Jocher and Chaurasia, 2024).
